concept.name: repeat(auto-fill, minmax())
concept.explain:
The 'repeat(auto-fill, minmax())' function allows you to create as many columns or rows as will fit in the container, each with a minimum and maximum size.
 The '1fr' unit allows tracks to grow and fill available space, while 'minmax(80px, 1fr)' ensures tracks are at least 80px wide but can expand to fill the container.

ui.prompt: Keep the car in column 1 / row 2 and resize tracks using auto-fill so it matches the highlighted wider bay.
hints: Do not change grid-row and grid-column.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-width: 80px; justify-self: stretch;

concept.name: auto-fill with row tracks
concept.explain: The 'repeat(auto-fill, minmax())' function can also be applied to rows, allowing you to create as many rows as will fit in the container, each with a minimum and maximum size.

ui.prompt: Keep the car in column 3 / row 1 and use auto-fill on rows so it matches the highlighted taller bay.
hints: Do not change grid-column and grid-row.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-height: 80px; align-self: stretch;

concept.name: auto-fit behavior
concept.explain:
The 'repeat(auto-fit, minmax())' function allows you to create as many columns or rows as will fit in the container, each with a minimum and maximum size.
The difference between auto-fit and auto-fill is that auto-fit will collapse empty tracks, while auto-fill will keep them.

ui.prompt: Keep the car in column 2 / row 2 and size both axes responsively to match the highlighted bay. Try auto-fit on columns and rows.
hints: Do not change grid-column and grid-row.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-width: 80px; min-height: 80px; justify-self: stretch; align-self: stretch;
